title: Avoid using File.deleteOnExit()
description: Using File.deleteOnExit() is not recommended due to potential issues with JVM shutdown, memory release, and file handling.
recommendation: Avoid using File.deleteOnExit() and use other methods for file deletion.
impact: Potential data loss, memory leaks, and security vulnerabilities.

noncompliant:
File file = new File("file.txt");
file.deleteOnExit();  // Noncompliant

checks.message: Avoid using File.deleteOnExit() - use explicit file deletion or try-with-resources instead
